title: Colorful milk snake found on the loose in the South End
author: John Waller
bullets: A milk snake was found loose in Boston's South End and is now at MSPCA-Angell's Adoption Center.
MSPCA-Angell is trying to locate the snake's owner before offering it for adoption.
Milk snakes require specific care and are known to be escape artists, advises MSPCA.

title: Unresponsive boy pulled from Andover pond
author: John Waller
bullets: A boy was pulled unresponsive from Field Pond in Andover's Harold Parker State Forest on Tuesday.
First responders used a paddle board and flotation devices to locate the boy 25 feet from shore.
The boy was medflighted to a Boston hospital after being initially taken to Lawrence General Hospital.

title: Where did Sandwich, Mass. get its name?
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Sandwich, Massachusetts likely got its name from Sandwich, Kent, in England, but the specific origin remains unclear.
John Humphrey, a colonial official with ties to Sandwich, Kent, may have influenced the naming of Sandwich, Mass.
Sandwich, Massachusetts was named in the 1600s, unrelated to the food item named after John Montagu, the fourth Earl of Sandwich.

title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (May 20)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: Sales in Abington range from $425,000 for a Cape Cod to $784,900 for a Colonial.
Acton's highest sale was a condo at 3 Squirrel Hill Road for $1,300,000.
Arlington's top sale was a two-family building at 19 Avon Place for $2,580,000.

title: MBTA announces new ferry service starting in June that will loop around Boston Harbor
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: The MBTA will launch the F10 'Harbor Loop' ferry service on June 29, connecting four Boston locations year-round.
Harbor Loop ferries will run every 30 minutes during peak hours with morning and evening services but no midday service.
Tickets cost $2.40 for full-fare and $1.10 for reduced fare; CharlieCards are not accepted on this route.

title: ‘The world responded’: More than a thousand people attend funeral for East Bridgewater veteran who left behind no family
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Over 1,500 people attended the funeral of WWII veteran John Bernard Arnold III in East Bridgewater.
Arnold, 98, had no known family, prompting a community-driven funeral coordinated by local veteran services.
Attendees traveled from across the country, gathering at St. Joseph the Worker Catholic Church for the service.

title: Revisiting our bold preseason predictions for 2025-26 Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins earned 100 points and made the playoffs in Marco Sturm's first year as coach.
Jeremy Swayman was a Vezina Trophy finalist, significantly improving after a difficult 2024-25 season.
Boston's power play improved under assistant coach Steve Spott, finishing ninth in the NHL with a 23.4% success rate.

title: 4 Rhode Island firefighters reinstated, 1 facing charges, after investigation into drug allegations
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Four Pawtucket firefighters reinstated after drug-related investigation; others challenged or accepted terminations.
One firefighter, Terrence O’Neill, was indicted and pleaded not guilty to drug-related charges.
Six firefighters were terminated, three retired, and one resigned amid investigation in Pawtucket, RI.

title: ‘You’d have to ask the voters’: Bill Belichick reacts to Pro Football Hall of Fame snub
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bill Belichick was not elected to the Pro Football Hall of Fame in his first year of eligibility.
Belichick has won eight Super Bowls, six with the Patriots and two with the Giants as defensive coordinator.
Belichick is now head coach at the University of North Carolina and will be eligible for 2027 Hall of Fame induction.

title: Person snuck into Harvard dorm, attacked student from behind, school says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: An unknown male entered Harvard's Lowell House and allegedly assaulted a student Tuesday evening.
The suspect followed someone into the residence hall, covered the victim's mouth, and attempted to force them into a room.
The victim screamed, causing the suspect to flee; authorities have launched an active criminal investigation.
